metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: I can't figure out why I'm so bothered
there are criticisms about my relationship
it's weird for a 58 year old man to be with me
age is an issue for others but not for me

neutral: Fear of reaction regarding family planning choices (finding)

llm_response.response:
REASONING:
"retrieved_concepts_used": ["automatic_thoughts", "emotions"]

Your feelings of being bothered might stem from automatic thoughts that question the validity of your relationship due to the age difference, and you may be experiencing emotions such as anxiety or confusion.

I'm here to help you explore these thoughts and feelings further. Let's start by understanding more about what you're thinking and feeling when you wake up with those thoughts. Can you tell me more about that?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess when I wake up, I often feel this weight on my chest. It’s like... I start questioning everything, you know? The age difference keeps nagging at me, and it makes me feel anxious, like maybe this isn’t meant to be or something. I just wish I could silence those doubts, but they always seem to creep back in.

"retrieved_concepts_used": ["emotions", "automatic thoughts"]

Your response:
I understand that you're feeling anxious when you wake up, and it seems like there are some doubts that keep coming back. Let's explore these feelings together and see if we can find a way to help you feel more at ease.

patient.query: I don’t know, it’s just overwhelming sometimes. I wake up and feel this tightness in my chest, like something’s off. The age difference keeps swirling around in my mind, making me anxious. I just wish I could push those doubts away, but they keep coming back, and it makes me question everything.

patient.query: Yeah, it's just hard to shake. I keep thinking about how people might judge us because of the age gap. It feels like everyone’s watching, and I worry that I’m making a mistake. Sometimes I just want to go back to that carefree feeling we had at the start, but now… it feels heavy.

patient.query: I don’t know... I guess a friend might say it doesn't matter as long as we’re happy. But it’s hard for me to see it that way. All I can think about is how people have reacted in the past, and it makes me feel like I need to defend us or something. I just want to feel secure without all this noise in my head.

automatic_thoughts: it's hard to see it that way

This is causing feelings of anxiety and insecurity. Let's explore these thoughts together and find a way to help you feel more secure without all this noise in your head.

patient.query: I don’t know... A part of me feels like it shouldn't matter if we’re happy, but I keep getting caught up in what others might think. It’s tough to shake off those past reactions I’ve faced, and it makes me feel like I need to justify everything. I just wish I could feel more secure without all this noise in my head.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s frustrating for sure. I just wish I could quiet the noise. I want to focus on what feels good between us, but it’s hard when those old feelings and doubts sneak back in. Sometimes, I just want to feel free to enjoy what we have without this pressure hanging over me.

patient.query: Yeah, it is overwhelming. I want to be present and just enjoy what we have, but these nagging doubts make it hard. Sometimes I feel like I’m chasing that freedom, but I keep getting pulled back into my worries. I just wish things felt lighter, you know?
